title: Add Decimals by Aligning Place Value
description: Add two decimal numbers by aligning decimal points and adding corresponding place values.
condition: Two or more decimal numbers to be added.
response: Line up decimal points, add by place value, and place the decimal point in the sum.
label: Principle
notes: Used in multi-step context problems as well.

title: Align Decimal Points
description: Line up decimal points so that digits of the same place value are in the same column before adding or subtracting.
condition: Two or more decimal numbers to be added or subtracted.
response: Rewrite numbers vertically so decimal points are directly aligned.
label: Concept
notes: Critical prerequisite for correct computation.

title: Append Trailing Zeros to Decimals
description: Add zeros to the right of a decimal number without changing its value to match place values.
condition: A decimal number has fewer decimal places than another number in the same calculation.
response: Add one or more zeros to the right of the decimal digits to match place value positions.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Based on place value equivalence (e.g., $0.3 = 0.30$).

title: Area Model for Decimal Multiplication
description: Use a decomposed rectangle (area model) to compute a product by summing partial areas.
condition: Decimal factors decomposed by place value in an area diagram.
response: Compute each partial area and sum them.
label: Principle
notes: Conceptual strategy alternative to algorithm.

title: Area of a Rectangle
description: Compute the area of a rectangle by multiplying its length by its width (or height).
condition: Given a rectangle with known length and width (or height).
response: Multiply length × width to find the area.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a rectangle has length $7.2$ m and height $3.8$ m, then area $= 7.2 \times 3.8 = 27.36$ square meters.

title: Compute Quotient Using Long Division
description: Carry out the full long division algorithm (divide, multiply, subtract, bring down) to compute a quotient.
condition: Given a dividend and divisor set up in long division format.
response: Iteratively divide, multiply, subtract, and bring down digits until the quotient is found.
label: Production / Skill
example: To compute $78.9 \div 2$, divide 7 by 2 (3), multiply $3\times2=6$, subtract to get 1, bring down 8, and continue until $39.45$ is obtained.
notes: Encapsulates the repeated procedural cycle of long division.

title: Convert Decimal to Fraction (Power of 10)
description: Rewrite a terminating decimal as a fraction with denominator a power of 10 based on place value.
condition: Given a terminating decimal number.
response: Express the decimal as an integer over $10^n$, where $n$ is the number of decimal places.
label: Principle / Model

title: Convert Improper Fraction to Mixed Number
description: Rewrite an improper fraction as a mixed number by dividing the numerator by the denominator.
condition: Given an improper fraction $\frac{a}{b}$ with $a>b$.
response: Divide $a$ by $b$ to express the result as a whole number and remainder, forming a mixed number.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Appears in solution explanation for expressing 1.6 as $1\frac{3}{5}$.

title: Create Equivalent Whole-Number Division Expression
description: Rewrite a decimal division problem as an equivalent problem with whole numbers by multiplying both numbers by the same power of 10.
condition: Given a division expression with decimals in the dividend and/or divisor.
response: Multiply both dividend and divisor by a power of 10 to form an equivalent division expression with whole numbers.
label: Principle / Model
example: $1.2 \div 0.75 = 120 \div 75$ because both numbers are multiplied by $100$.
notes: Uses the principle that multiplying numerator and denominator by the same nonzero number preserves quotient value.

title: Estimate Decimal Product by Rounding
description: Estimate a product of decimals by rounding to compatible numbers and multiplying.
condition: A product of decimals where an estimate is requested.
response: Round factors to convenient numbers and multiply to estimate.
label: Principle
notes: Used to select closest multiple-choice estimate.

title: Evaluate and Compare to Threshold
description: Evaluate numerical expressions and determine whether their values exceed a given number.
condition: Several expressions and a comparison value (e.g., greater than 5).
response: Compute each expression and select those exceeding the threshold.
label: Principle
notes: Requires computation plus comparison.

title: Find Percent of Quantity Using Decimal
description: Convert a percent to a decimal and multiply to find the part of a quantity.
condition: A percent and a base quantity are given.
response: Convert percent to decimal and multiply by the quantity.
label: Principle
notes: Used to compute discounts.

title: Interpret Decimal Place Value
description: Understand the value of digits based on their position relative to the decimal point.
condition: A decimal number with digits in tenths, hundredths, etc.
response: State or use the value of a digit according to its place (e.g., 0.49 < 0.5).
label: Principle
example: In 0.49, the 9 is in the hundredths place, so $0.49 < 0.5$.
notes: Supports comparison and correct computation.

title: Interpret Division as a Fraction
description: Rewrite a division expression as a fraction with the dividend as numerator and divisor as denominator.
condition: Given a division expression $a \div b$.
response: Rewrite as the fraction $\frac{a}{b}$.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Supports reasoning about quotient size and simplification.

title: Multiply Decimals Using Standard Algorithm
description: Multiply decimal numbers using the standard multiplication algorithm.
condition: Two decimal factors to multiply.
response: Multiply as with whole numbers and adjust decimal placement.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Often executed procedurally without full verbalization.

title: Multiply Whole Numbers Using Standard Algorithm
description: Use the standard multiplication algorithm to compute the product of multi-digit whole numbers.
condition: Given two whole numbers to multiply.
response: Apply the standard algorithm (partial products and addition) to find the product.
label: Production / Skill
notes: Algorithmic skill; explanation not required for execution.

title: Place Decimal in Product
description: Determine the position of the decimal point in a product based on total decimal places in factors.
condition: Product of two decimals computed as whole numbers first.
response: Place decimal so total decimal places equals sum of decimal places in factors.
label: Principle
notes: Core procedural rule for decimal multiplication.

title: Place Decimal Point in Quotient
description: Place the decimal point in the quotient directly above the decimal point in the dividend during long division by a whole number.
condition: Dividing a decimal by a whole number using long division.
response: Write the decimal point in the quotient aligned vertically with the decimal point in the dividend.
label: Category
example: In $78.9 \div 2$, place the decimal point in the quotient directly above the decimal in 78.9 to get $39.45$.
notes: Often applied procedurally without verbal justification.

title: Regroup in Decimal Addition
description: Compose a unit of higher place value when a column sum is 10 or more in decimal addition.
condition: Sum of digits in a place value column is 10 or greater during decimal addition.
response: Write the ones digit in the column and add the tens digit to the next column to the left.
label: Principle / Model
example: In $0.076 + 0.850$, hundredths: $7 + 5 = 12$ hundredths, write $2$ hundredths and regroup $1$ tenth.
notes: Applies across whole and decimal places.

title: Regroup in Decimal Subtraction
description: Decompose a unit from a higher place value when the top digit is smaller than the bottom digit in subtraction.
condition: In a subtraction column, the minuend digit is less than the subtrahend digit.
response: Borrow 1 unit from the next place to the left, convert it to 10 of the current place, and subtract.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires understanding of place value decomposition.

title: Scale Product by Powers of Ten
description: Adjust a whole-number product when factors are scaled by powers of 10.
condition: One or both factors are multiplied or divided by powers of 10 relative to whole numbers.
response: Multiply or divide the original product by the corresponding power of 10.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Decimals by Aligning Place Value
description: Subtract one decimal from another by aligning decimal points and subtracting by place value.
condition: A subtraction expression involving decimals.
response: Align decimal points, subtract by place value, and place the decimal point in the difference.
label: Principle
notes: May involve regrouping across decimal places.

title: Use Ten-Times Relationship
description: If one quantity is 10 times another, divide by 10 (or multiply by 1/10) to find the smaller quantity.
condition: Statement that one quantity is 10 times another and the larger value is known.
response: Divide the larger quantity by 10 or multiply by 1/10.
label: Principle
example: If A = 50 and A is 10×B, then $B = 50 \div 10 = 5$.
notes: Equivalent to multiplying by $\frac{1}{10}$.
